data.title: ARE: FFO per share diluted as adjusted reached $1.73, with occupancy at 87.7% and leasing set to rebound
data.description:
FFO per share diluted as adjusted was $1.73 for 1Q 2026, with full-year guidance midpoint reaffirmed at $6.40. Occupancy fell to 87.7% due to known expirations, but leasing is expected to rebound next quarter. Dispositions and capital allocation remain on track despite ongoing market headwinds.

data.title: Expand Energy beats Q1 revenue estimates, maintains 2026 production forecast
data.description:
Overview

  • US natural gas producer's Q1 revenue rose, beating analyst expectations

  • Adjusted EPS for Q1 beat analyst expectations

  • Company repurchased $150 mln in stock and reduced net debt by $1.6 bln

Outlook

  • Expand Energy reaffirms 2026 full-year production guidance of ~7.5 Bcfe/d

  • Company expects to run 11 to 12 rigs and invest ~$2.85 bln in 2026

  • Expand Energy sees strong global demand for natural gas and LNG

Result Drivers

  • HIGHER PRODUCTION - Q1 net production rose to ~7.44 Bcfe/d, supported by 13 rigs and 49 wells turned in line

  • IMPROVED REALIZED PRICES - Realized natural gas prices increased to $4.28/Mcf from $3.51/Mcf a year earlier

  • OPERATIONAL SCALE - Co cited its scale and operational discipline as supporting cash generation and returns

data.title: CareDx Q1 revenue rises 39%, beats estimates
data.description:
Overview

  • US transplant diagnostics firm's Q1 revenue rose 39%, beating analyst expectations

  • Company raised 2026 revenue and adjusted EBITDA guidance, announced $100 mln share buyback

Outlook

  • CareDx raises 2026 revenue guidance to $447 mln to $465 mln

  • Company raises 2026 adjusted EBITDA guidance to $43 mln to $57 mln

Result Drivers

  • TESTING SERVICES GROWTH - Higher testing volumes, increased average revenue per test, and improved collections drove 48% year-over-year growth in testing services revenue

  • PATIENT AND DIGITAL SOLUTIONS - Revenue rose 33% year-over-year, supported by adoption of new transplant centers and increased use of digital solutions

  • OPERATIONAL EXECUTION - Record cash collections and focus on high-margin testing services contributed to profitability

data.title: Booking Holdings expects Middle East conflict to impact bookings through June
data.description:
Overview

  • Online travel agency's Q1 revenue grew 16% yr/yr, slightly beating analyst expectations

  • Adjusted EPS for Q1 rose 14% and beat analyst expectations

  • Company repurchased $3.6 bln of stock in Q1

Outlook

  • Booking Holdings expects Q2 2026 revenue growth of 4%-6% year-over-year

  • Company forecasts full-year 2026 revenue growth in high single digits

  • Booking Holdings assumes Middle East conflict impacts bookings through June, recovery in H2 2026

Result Drivers

  • ROOM NIGHT GROWTH - Room nights grew 6% yr/yr, though growth was negatively impacted by the conflict in the Middle East

  • GROSS BOOKINGS INCREASE - Gross bookings rose 15% yr/yr, with the Middle East conflict estimated to have reduced growth in line with room night growth

  • ALTERNATIVE ACCOMMODATION GROWTH - Alternative accommodation room nights at Booking.com increased by 5.5% yr/yr

data.title: SeaStar Medical case dismissed with prejudice after stipulated dismissal
data.description:
SeaStar Medical said a putative class action alleging misstatements tied to its restatement has been dismissed with prejudice.

Key Highlights:

  • Putative class action Wells v. SeaStar Medical alleged securities fraud related to a March 27, 2024 restatement.
  • Plaintiff sought compensatory damages under Section 10(b), Rule 10b-5 and Section 20(a).
  • Parties filed a Stipulation of Dismissal with Prejudice on April 21, 2026; court ordered dismissal on April 27, 2026.

Original SEC Filing: SeaStar Medical Holding Corp [ ICU ] - 8-K - Apr. 28, 2026

Disclaimer
This is an AI-powered summary. It may contain inaccuracies. Consider verifying important information with the source. Please note this summary is solely based on documents filed with the SEC.

data.title: CoStar Group slightly beats Q1 revenue estimates, reaffirms 2026 revenue guidance
data.description:

  • Adjusted EPS for Q1 rose 53% to $0.23, beating analyst expectations

Outlook

  • Company raises 2026 adjusted EBITDA guidance to $780 mln-$820 mln

  • NET NEW BOOKINGS - Co reported $67 mln in net new bookings in Q1, up 20% yr/yr, supporting revenue growth

  • DIGITAL PLATFORM ENGAGEMENT - Co said customer engagement accelerated with best-ever time on site, page views, and bounce rates

data.title: Starbucks raises forecasts after strong quarter as CEO's turnaround gains momentum
data.description:
Starbucks beat Wall Street estimates for quarterly comparable sales and raised its annual forecasts, signaling investments in faster service and improved staffing under CEO Brian Niccol's efforts to turn around the coffee chain pulled in more customers.

The company's shares jumped 6.7% in extended trading on Tuesday. The stock has gained about 15% this year.

Niccol's strategy to refocus Starbucks on in-store execution through measures like a simplified menu and shortened wait times has brought back consumers in its core U.S. market. He has paired that with the "Back to Starbucks" initiative, which includes improvements to worker compensation to boost employee retention and consistency on the floor.

The world's largest coffee chain reported a 6.2% increase in global same‑store sales for the second quarter, above analysts' expectations of a 3.7% rise, according to data compiled by LSEG.

The company projected fiscal 2026 adjusted profit per share to be $2.25 to $2.45, compared to its previous expectations of between $2.15 and $2.4.

It forecast fiscal 2026 global same-store sales to increase 5%, above its prior estimates of a 3% rise or higher.

"Around the world, we're getting leaner and moving faster. We're holding ourselves accountable to clear standards. And clearly we are innovating with discipline. That focus is driving better execution. And, in turn, better results," Niccol said in a statement.

Overall visits to Starbucks rose 5.5% in the quarter, according to Placer.ai data.

The company's quarterly consolidated operating margin was 9.4%, rising 120 basis points from the prior year.

data.title: Omnicom Q1 revenue beats estimates on IPG acquisition
data.description:
Overview

  • Marketing and sales firm's Q1 revenue beat analyst expectations, driven by IPG acquisition and organic growth

  • Q1 adjusted EPS beat analyst expectations, up 12% yr/yr

  • ORGANIC GROWTH & CURRENCY - Organic revenue grew 3.9% and foreign currency translation added 2.7% to core operations revenue

data.title: Robinhood quarterly profit rises on trading strength
data.description:
Robinhood Markets reported a rise in first-quarter profit on Tuesday, as market volatility boosted trading activity at the online brokerage.

Profit was $346 million, or 38 cents per share, in the three months ended March 31, compared with $336 million, or 37 cents per share, a year earlier.

data.description: Apr 28 (Reuters) - Visa Q2 EPS USD 3.14.

data.title: Visa quarterly profit rises on robust card spending volumes
data.description:
Visa reported a higher second-quarter profit on Tuesday as the world's largest payment processor benefited from a steady rise in payment volumes despite ongoing macroeconomic uncertainty.

The company's adjusted net income came in at $6.3 billion, or $3.31 per share, in the three months ended March 31 compared with $5.44 billion or $2.76 per share a year earlier.

data.title: Key facts: GM trims tariffs; raises guidance; records supplier claims
data.description:
• GM cut 2026 tariff cost outlook to $2.5–$3.5B after a Supreme Court ruling. North America adjusted EBIT rose to $3.661B; GMI EBIT added $123M; China equity income added $165M.1
  • GM Q1: adjusted EBIT $4.3B, revenue $43.6B, adjusted EPS $3.70; repurchases $0.8B, dividends $0.2B. Raised 2026 adjusted EBIT guidance to $13.5–$15.5B and EPS to $11.50–$13.50.234
  • GM reported it has recognized 90% of expected supplier commercial claim costs tied to scaled-back EV projects and expects most related cash outflows by year-end, affecting near-term cash forecasts.5
  • GM holds a 42% U.S. full‑size pickup market share; fleet deliveries rose 8%, keeping GM the top U.S. fleet seller as demand for pickups and vans grew.6
  • GM says its hands-free driving and digital services scale drives higher volumes and more deferred and realized revenue than rivals; targets 13 million digital subscribers by year-end.7
  • GM will add Google Gemini to 2022‑and‑newer Cadillac, Chevrolet, Buick and GMC models, affecting in‑car software and infotainment across those brands for vehicles from model year 2022 onward.8

data.title: Key facts: FCC reviews 8 Disney ABC licenses; Trump seeks Kimmel ouster
data.description:
• FCC will begin early reviews of eight ABC broadcast licenses owned by Disney (DIS), moved up from Oct 2028 after a probe into Disney/ABC diversity practices; reviews may lead to revocation.1
  • Disney (DIS) facing political pressure after Trump called for firing ABC host Jimmy Kimmel; no Disney response yet. Traders may note potential reputational risk as FCC reviews related licensing.234

data.title: Key facts: Energy Transfer Q1 Results May 5; Raises Distribution 0.7%
data.description:
• Energy Transfer (ET) will report Q1 2026 results May 5 before market open and hold a May 5 conference call at 8:00 a.m. CT / 9:00 a.m. ET; call will be webcast on company site.1
  • Energy Transfer (ET) raised its quarterly cash distribution 0.7% to $0.3375/share, annualized $1.35. Payable May 20 to holders of record May 8. Implied yield ~7.1% at $19.05 close.2

data.title: Key facts: EPD Q1 record EBITDA; raised guidance; Mentone West online
data.description:
• EPD Q1 2026: gas processing inlet 8.3 Bcf/d (+7% YoY); NGL fractionation 1.9 MMBPD (+16%); marine terminals 2.3 MMBPD (+15%). Mentone West 2 online; two 300 MMcf/d plants planned for 2027.1
  • EPD posted Q1 2026: record EBITDA and operating volumes, gross margin up, Adjusted EBITDA +10%, net income +6%. New assets and stronger NGL/gas exports drove results; raised 2026–27 growth guidance.23

data.title: Key facts: Coca‑Cola Q1 rev +11–12% (vol +3%); Braun CEO; Coke Zero +13%
data.description:
• Coca‑Cola Q1 2026: revenue ~$12.48B (+11–12%), organic revenue +10%, volume +3%, adj. EPS $0.86 (+18%), operating margin 35.0%, free cash flow $1.76B, raised FY EPS growth guide 8–9%.123
  • Coca-Cola names Henrique Braun CEO March 31; strategy targets affordability via packaging and pricing—focus on 1.25L bottles and mini cans to match daily consumer spending; no discount push4
  • Coca‑Cola (KO) volume highlights: Coke Zero Sugar +13%, tea +8%, water +5%, sparkling beverages +2%. Growth aided by Fairlife and Santa Clara; Nigeria divestiture trimmed comparable base.5
  • Coca‑Cola (KO) facing aluminum can shortage in India; supply disruption noted. CFO says company is supporting operations and a procurement team is working to resolve the issue soon.6
  • Coca‑Cola (KO) reports steady consumer demand. Management hedged some lower input costs and is working with bottlers to manage rising plastic and aluminum packaging expenses and related operations.7

data.title: Key facts: Chevron Q4 revenue $46.87B; MoU with Libya on shale gas and oil
data.description:
• Chevron (CVX) Q4 revenue $46.87B, down 10.2% YoY; EPS and EBITDA beat estimates by 2.6%. Shares up ~8.1% post-results, trading near $184.97.1
  • Chevron (CVX) signed an MoU with Libya’s National Oil Corporation to study shale oil and gas; preliminary potential cited: ~123 trillion cubic feet gas and ~18 billion barrels oil.2

data.title: Key facts: Cadence unveils AgentStack; Q1 rev +19% $1.47B; targets up
data.description:
• Cadence (CDNS) launched AgentStack and AI Super Agents across RTL, verification, analog and digital flows. Core EDA revenue rose 18% YoY; IP business grew 22% with wider Star IP adoption.1
  • Cadence (CDNS) Q1 FY2026: revenue $1.474B (+19%), billings $1.49B (+21%), GAAP EPS $1.23, adj. EPS $1.96. Backlog $8.0B, RPO $4.0B. FY26 revenue guide $6.125–6.225B; non-GAAP EPS $7.85–7.95.234
  • Piper Sandler raised Cadence (CDNS) target to $325 from $319; KeyBanc lifted its target to $425 from $405. Both firms bumped their price targets for CDNS shares.5

data.title: Key facts: Broadcom AI $8.4B Q1; OpenAI missed targets, warned funding risk
data.description:
• Broadcom AI revenue hit $8.4B in Q1, up 106% y/y. Q2 AI semiconductor revenue guidance set at $10.7B. Figures reflect reported Q1 results and next‑quarter AI chip guidance.1
  • OpenAI said it missed internal user targets and warned weaker sales could curb funding for compute—creating headwinds for Broadcom (AVGO), a key supplier to AI chip partners.2

data.title: Key facts: Analyst Lifts AMD 2026 Forecast; Advancing AI on July 23
data.description:
• Analyst lifted AMD’s 2026 revenue and gross profit forecasts, noted possible price rises if demand outstrips supply, and cited Meta’s data‑center expansion and OpenAI fundraising as demand drivers.1
  • AMD to host Advancing AI 2026 July 23 in San Francisco and livestreamed. Event will showcase AMD’s AI chips, software, systems and feature leadership, partners, customers and developers.2

data.title: Key facts: Alphabet 2026 CapEx $175–185B; Amends DoD AI deal; Earnings
data.description:
• Alphabet says 2026 CapEx will be $175–$185B due to AI compute demand; management signals higher 2027 spending possible and won't update 2027 guidance this earnings season.1
  • Alphabet amended a contract letting the U.S. Department of Defense use Google AI models for classified and lawful purposes, permit safety-setting tweaks, and retain Google operational control.234
  • Alphabet (GOOG) reports earnings this week. Traders will watch management comments on AI guidance and capex, plus any disclosed AI spending, as shares trade near record highs.5
  • Pentagon added Alphabet’s Gemini 3.1 Pro and 3.0 Flash to GenAI.mil. Models aim to cut hallucinations, boost output quality and productivity for military users. Provider: Alphabet (GOOG).6
  • EU DMA draft would force Google to open Android APIs and let rival AI, including Gemini-based services, access apps and device functions; Google warns of privacy, security and cost risks.7
  • EU expands DMA to cover AI and cloud. Regulators may designate AI assistant core platform services and assess cloud gatekeepers; Alphabet (GOOG) is under review for possible cloud gatekeeper status.8
  • Google (GOOG) is set to report Q1 revenue growth near 18.6% year‑over‑year, accelerating from about 12% a year earlier; analyst estimates for the quarter were stable over the past 30 days.9
  • Google unveiled two new TPUs and agentic AI apps for Gemini at Cloud Next, noted ongoing Cloud and Gemini upgrades, and said Gemini exceeds 750 million monthly active users.10
  • Google (GOOG) is reviewing an Australian draft 2.25% local-revenue levy on big tech that don’t strike news payment deals; Google says it opposes the tax and cites existing news licensing agreements.11
  • Google lobbied against California’s “Based Act.” The bill, targeting platform self-preference in apps and search, failed to advance after a key committee vote on April 20.12
